ekern_transcription.label: Full kern transcription with embedded metadata
ekern_transcription.expectation: Metadata and structure expected to succeed; exact pitches may hallucinate. Output uses music21-native kern (*> section labels, !!!OMD: tempo) for direct rendering via LilyPond (kern_to_ly pipeline).
ekern_transcription.prompt:
Transcribe this piano sheet music into a complete **kern file. Output ONLY the kern file — no prose, no explanation, no code fences.

RULES:
- Every data row has exactly TWO tab-separated columns: left=bass clef, right=treble clef.
- *> section lines must appear in BOTH columns: *>Label TAB *>Label
- Barlines must also have two columns: =1<TAB>=1

CONCRETE EXAMPLE — illustrates FORMAT only, not content (TAB = actual tab character between the two columns):
!!!OTL: Title exactly as printed
!!!COM: Full composer attribution line exactly as printed
!!!OMD: Andante                <- opening tempo word read from image
!!!YEC: Copyright line if visible, else omit
**kern TAB **kern
*clefF4 TAB *clefG2
*k[f#] TAB *k[f#]       <- read key from image: *k[]=C, *k[f#]=G, *k[b-]=F, etc.
*M4/4 TAB *M4/4         <- read time signature from image
*>Section_A TAB *>Section_A  <- section label; use underscore for spaces; read name from image
=1 TAB =1
2G 4D TAB 4d 4f# 4a
4G 4D TAB 2g
=2 TAB =2
...
*k[b-b-] TAB *k[b-b-]       <- if key changes mid-piece, emit new *k[] here
*>Section_B TAB *>Section_B  <- new section label read from image
=9 TAB =9
4A 4E TAB 4a 4cc
...
*- TAB *-

PITCH GUIDE:
- Uppercase = below middle C: C D E F G A B (one octave below)
  Double uppercase = two octaves below: CC DD FF GG (e.g. CC = low C)
- Lowercase = above middle C: c d e f g a b
  Double lowercase = two octaves above: cc dd ee ff gg
- Durations: 1=whole 2=half 4=quarter 8=eighth 16=sixteenth
- Rests: r (e.g. 4r = quarter rest)
- Beaming: L=beam start, J=beam end (e.g. 8cL 8dJ)
- Chords: space-separated (e.g. 4C 4E 4G = C major chord)
- Repeat barline: =:|!

Now transcribe the actual score image. Read the title, composer, copyright, section names, key, and tempo directly from the image. If the key changes mid-piece, emit the new *k[] record at that point. Transcribe as many measures as you can read accurately.
ekern_transcription.response:
